name: Moxifloxacin
executiveSummary: Antibacterial for treatment of drug-resistant tuberculosis.
indications:
## Main indications

Antibacterial for treatment of drug-resistant tuberculosis.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

pediatrico.dose: Infants and children < 15 years: 10 mg/kg/day every 24 h, maximum dose: 400 mg; adolescents and > 15 years: 400 mg every 24 h.
pediatrico.notes: Precautions: monitor for signs of tendinitis or tendon rupture; rupture risk increases with concomitant corticosteroid use. Use with caution in patients with CNS disorders because quinolones may cause seizures. Caution with drugs that lower K levels (e.g. loop and thiazide diuretics, laxatives, corticosteroids, amphotericin B) or associated with clinically significant bradycardia. Interactions: additive effect on QT prolongation when administered simultaneously with drugs that may affect QT. Space 6 h from antacids with Mg and Al, sucralfate, didanosine, and agents with Fe and Zn. Increases effect of oral anticoagulants; increased INR monitoring. See tuberculosis treatment guide.

stability:
## Stability

- IV: use per bag or dilution package insert.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Superinfections due to resistant bacteria or fungi, such as oral and vaginal candidiasis; headache, dizziness, insomnia, confusion, agitation, hallucinations; QT prolongation in patients with hypokalemia; nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain and diarrhea; increased transaminases.
